days.summary: A large Tauri-focused feature drop expanded the application shell, settings, integrations, terminal demos, issue tracking, workspace operations, real-time status surfaces, chat UI, and Theia communication support. The changes also introduced new assets, documentation, API wrappers, and many Codebase workspace components to support a richer desktop workflow.
frontendChangeBullets: Expanded the ORGII desktop shell with richer workspace navigation so users could move between home, settings, integrations, and active work areas more naturally.
Introduced broader settings and integrations surfaces, giving the desktop experience more visible places to configure connected tools and app behavior.
Added new terminal demo and rich-text interaction surfaces that made hands-on workspace workflows easier to explore inside the app.
Improved chat and real-time status UI so users could see more responsive feedback while working through project and workspace tasks.
Added issue-tracking and operation-center foundations in the UI, creating a clearer path from project context into actionable work management screens.
Introduced many workspace components for the Codebase experience, making the desktop app feel more like a complete project command center.
backendChangeBullets: Expanded the Tauri application foundation that supports the richer desktop shell, including runtime wiring for settings, integrations, workspace operations, and status surfaces.
Added API wrapper groundwork so frontend workspace components could call into desktop and integration functionality through clearer runtime boundaries.
Improved embedded IDE communication support, allowing workspace context to flow more reliably between ORGII and the embedded development environment.
Added supporting assets and documentation that helped organize the new desktop runtime, integration, and workspace-operation capabilities.
Strengthened the runtime base for terminal, chat, issue-tracking, and operation-center features introduced across the Codebase workspace.
Laid backend-facing foundations for real-time status surfaces so desktop workflows could expose live state rather than only static views.

days.summary: Mock data was added for the Issue Tracker, likely supporting local development and UI iteration for issue, module, session, and work item views introduced around the Codebase home experience.
frontendChangeBullets: Added realistic issue tracker sample content so the new issue, module, session, and work item screens could be viewed with meaningful data.
Improved local UI iteration for the Codebase home experience by making project-management screens feel populated during development.
Expanded visible scenarios for issue tracker lists and detail views, helping validate how modules and work items appear together.
Made session and work item views easier to evaluate by supplying sample records that resemble real project activity.
Supported faster product polish on recently introduced project-management surfaces by giving designers and developers richer UI states to inspect.
backendChangeBullets: Added structured mock data for issue tracker entities, giving the app a predictable local data source for development and testing.
Covered issue, module, session, and work item relationships in the sample dataset so data-driven views could be exercised together.
Improved development support for project-management data flows without requiring live service data for every UI pass.
Provided local test coverage for recently introduced Codebase home screens that depend on issue tracker records.
Created a safer basis for validating list, detail, and cross-entity rendering behavior against consistent issue tracker input.

days.summary: The UI was refined across the Codebase home, Atlas IDE, configuration, project history, session history, sidebar, and new workspace window surfaces. Work focused on navigation polish, icon updates, session creation, layout adjustments, and fixes to recently introduced home and history views.
frontendChangeBullets: Refined Codebase home navigation with cleaner layout and icon updates, making the first-run path into projects feel more polished.
Improved session creation screens so users could start work from the home and workspace flows with clearer visual guidance.
Updated session history views to make previous work easier to find, scan, and restart from the desktop app.
Cleaned up project history and configuration surfaces after the initial desktop rollout, reducing rough edges in recently added screens.
Polished Atlas IDE and workspace window surfaces so the transition from home into an active workspace felt more coherent.
Adjusted sidebar and global layout details to better support the expanding desktop workflow.
backendChangeBullets: Strengthened session creation flow wiring so new sessions could be launched more consistently from the updated UI surfaces.
Refined history data handling for project and session views, supporting more reliable presentation of recent work.
Updated workspace window support behind the newly polished desktop surfaces, improving how new work areas are opened and controlled.
Improved configuration flow foundations that connect the UI settings experience with desktop app behavior.
Stabilized recently introduced home and history features by addressing data and runtime assumptions surfaced during UI cleanup.
Continued aligning Atlas IDE and workspace window runtime behavior with the growing Codebase desktop experience.

days.summary: Tray menu and window-management capabilities were added alongside supporting documentation, icons, and Tauri dependencies. The new empty workspace window and session history table received additional UI work, while app and menu initialization utilities were introduced for desktop menu behavior.
frontendChangeBullets: Added system tray menu support so users could access and control the ORGII desktop app from a native desktop entry point.
Improved desktop window management flows for opening workspace windows and moving from the app shell into focused work areas.
Polished the empty workspace window, making the experience of starting a new workspace cleaner and less abrupt.
Updated the session history table so users could navigate previous sessions with a smoother, more desktop-native presentation.
Added supporting icons and app assets that made the tray, menu, and window-management experience feel more integrated.
Improved menu-driven desktop behavior so common app actions were available outside the main window.
backendChangeBullets: Added Tauri tray menu capabilities, giving the desktop runtime support for native system tray actions.
Introduced window-management capability wiring so workspace windows could be created and opened through controlled desktop APIs.
Added app and menu initialization utilities to support native menu behavior during desktop startup.
Updated Tauri dependencies needed for tray, menu, and window-management functionality.
Expanded capability configuration for desktop window flows, helping ensure new windows had the permissions they needed.
Added supporting documentation and setup work around the new native desktop runtime features.

days.summary: Home, dashboard, session history, project selection, and launched-workspace screens were improved and corrected after the tray and window updates. The work also touched global styling, modal styling, workspace session synchronization, and Tauri capability configuration for the desktop UI flow.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved home, dashboard, and launched-workspace screens so users had clearer entry points into projects and active work.
Enhanced session history views to make previous workspace activity easier to resume from the desktop app.
Refined repository and project selection flows, improving the steps users take before opening or continuing work.
Fixed workspace panel layout issues so active sessions and desktop panels behaved more predictably after launch.
Polished modal, dropdown, and global styling for a more consistent experience across home and workspace screens.
Adjusted launched-workspace presentation after tray and window updates, making the desktop flow feel more connected.
backendChangeBullets: Fixed workspace session synchronization issues so active workspace state stayed aligned with the desktop UI.
Updated Tauri capability configuration for the desktop flow introduced around tray menus and workspace windows.
Strengthened runtime support for repository selection and launched-workspace flows used before session startup.
Improved state handling around session history and project selection so resumed work could load more reliably.
Continued stabilizing window and panel behavior after the new desktop window-management work.
Aligned backend-facing desktop configuration with the updated home, dashboard, and workspace entry paths.

days.summary: The new empty workspace window received a targeted UI fix, continuing the late-June polishing work around the desktop window creation flow.
frontendChangeBullets: Polished the empty workspace window used when starting a new desktop workspace.
Fixed visual issues in the workspace creation flow, making the new-workspace launch feel cleaner.
Improved the late-June desktop window creation experience with a targeted UI cleanup.
Reduced rough edges in the empty-state experience users see before a workspace has active project content.
Made the transition into a new workspace feel more deliberate by tightening the presentation of the empty workspace screen.
backendChangeBullets: Stabilized the desktop window creation flow that backs the empty workspace experience.
Adjusted runtime-facing window behavior enough to support the targeted empty-workspace UI fix.
Continued hardening the new workspace startup path introduced during the late-June desktop work.
Kept the empty workspace flow aligned with the surrounding Tauri window-management setup.
Improved confidence in the initial workspace launch path by addressing a focused issue in the new desktop window surface.
